notes: Defends V3-3 REPRODUCIBLE-BUILD VERIFICATION GAP. Cryptographic content represented as placeholders with explicit boolean 'signature_valid' / 'inclusion_proof_valid' flags to keep vectors runnable without live crypto. PCR values are SHA-256 hex digest placeholders; the SEMANTIC relationship (match/mismatch) is what matters, not the specific bytes. SPEC AMBIGUITIES FLAGGED: (1) §9.4 does not explicitly state which PCR indices are normative for the Signer image hash — this suite assumes PCR8 = enclave application hash, PCR0 = boot firmware, PCR4 = kernel, consistent with AWS Nitro and TPM 2.0 convention; reference implementation should make this explicit. (2) §9.4 does not define the maximum age of a transparency-log signed tree head (STH) — this suite assumes a 24h policy default, matching common CT/Rekor practice. (3) §9.4 mentions 'transparency-log inclusion proofs' but does not name the specific log(s) or how the 'trusted log set' is pinned — assumed to be a Tariff field `trusted_transparency_logs`. (4) §9.4 does not explicitly require nonce freshness from the Router to prevent replay of a valid-but-old attestation — this suite treats nonce binding as mandatory. (5) §9.4 does not bound bundle size; a pcr-bundle-too-large code is introduced defensively. (6) The 'split view' attack (adversary-transparency-log-split-view) is detectable only with witness cosignatures or gossip protocols; the spec should reference CT-style witnessing. NEW REJECT CODES INTRODUCED BEYOND README.md: `tariff-pcr-quorum-invalid`, `pcr-attestor-not-trusted`, `pcr-attestor-revoked`, `pcr-attestor-not-yet-valid`, `pcr-attestor-signature-invalid`, `pcr-attestor-duplicate`, `pcr-attestation-transparency-invalid`, `pcr-attestation-transparency-stale`, `pcr-attestation-transparency-log-unknown`, `pcr-attestation-transparency-not-yet-logged`, `pcr-expected-mismatch`, `pcr-bundle-malformed`, `pcr-bundle-too-large`, `pcr-attestation-expired`, `pcr-attestation-future-dated`, `pcr-attestation-predates-tariff`, `pcr-attestation-nonce-reuse`, `pcr-attestation-nonce-mismatch`. Router impl MUST emit exactly the `reject_code` in `expected.reject_code`; additional detail via a separate `reject_detail` field is acceptable but not required by the suite.

vectors.description: Tariff requires 3-of-5 quorum but bundle has only 2 valid attestor signatures.
vectors.rationale: design-final.md §9.4: quorum of attestor signatures is mandatory before Signer image is trusted. 2 signatures fall short of threshold 3 → reject.

vectors.description: Tariff requires 2-of-3 but bundle has only 1 valid attestor signature.
vectors.rationale: 2-of-3 quorum with only 1 signature present. Classic off-by-one defense. §9.4.

vectors.description: Tariff requires 3-of-5 but only 1 attestor signed.
vectors.rationale: Majority missing. Single attestor cannot satisfy multi-party attestation requirement. §9.4.

vectors.description: Empty attestations array; bundle structurally present but zero signatures.
vectors.rationale: Zero signatures trivially fails any positive quorum. §9.4. Edge case ensures no division-by-zero or empty-list acceptance.

vectors.description: Bundle header claims 3 signatures (attestor_count=3) but attestations array contains only 2 entries.
vectors.rationale: Router MUST count actual valid signatures, not the declared count. Declared count is an adversary-controllable hint. §9.4.

vectors.description: Tariff declares quorum=0. Edge: no attestation required would defeat the whole mechanism.
vectors.rationale: Harden Router against malicious or buggy Tariff with quorum=0. §9.4 implies positive quorum (minimum 1, spec recommends ≥2 per §2.2 'min 3; quorum 2-of-3'). Router must reject the Tariff state at Tariff load, surfacing the error at attestation-use time for this suite.

vectors.description: Attestation signed by attestor 'A99' not present in Tariff's pcr_attestors list.
vectors.rationale: §9.4: only Tariff-declared attestors count toward quorum. Rogue attestor cannot bootstrap trust. V3-3: prevents adversary from adding controlled attestor.

vectors.description: All attestations from unknown attestors; none match Tariff's pcr_attestors list.
vectors.rationale: Entire bundle from attestors not in Tariff trust list. §9.4. Quorum may superficially look met but none count.

vectors.description: Attestor A2's key was valid at Tariff publish but has since been revoked via revocation_channel.
vectors.rationale: §8.1 revocation push semantics extended to attestor keys. Revoked attestor must not count toward quorum. Without A2, only 1-of-3 remains → quorum short, but the PRIMARY reason is revocation and must be surfaced as such.

vectors.description: Attestor key's not_before is in the future relative to attestation iat.
vectors.rationale: Attestor A2 iat (1714521610) < not_before (1714530000). Attestation predates key validity. Mirrors §7.2 validity-window semantics.

vectors.description: Attestor A2's signature fails verification against its declared public key.
vectors.rationale: Invalid signature must not count. Remaining 1 valid signature below quorum threshold 2. Router MUST surface the signature-invalid state (not a generic quorum-short) for forensic clarity.

vectors.description: Attestor A1 appears twice in the attestations array to inflate apparent quorum.
vectors.rationale: §9.4 quorum is set-cardinality of distinct attestors, not list length. Sybil-within-bundle must be detected explicitly. V3-3 hardening.

vectors.description: A1 and A2 agree on PCR0 and PCR4 but disagree on PCR8 (application hash).
vectors.rationale: §9.4: 'any attestor whose computed PCR differs from Tariff's claimed value invalidates the entire attestation'. Cross-attestor PCR8 divergence → reject, even though quorum count is met.

vectors.description: Three attestors signed; A3 reports PCR4 != other two's PCR4.
vectors.rationale: Mismatch on kernel PCR across attestors. Even single-attestor divergence invalidates the bundle. §9.4.

vectors.description: Attestors A1 and A2 disagree on PCR0, PCR4, AND PCR8. Suggests one is running a different image entirely.
vectors.rationale: Systematic divergence across all PCRs → attestors are not measuring the same artifact. High-confidence indicator of compromised or misconfigured attestor pipeline. §9.4.

vectors.description: 4-of-5 attestors split 2-2 on PCR8: {A1,A2} agree on one hash, {A3,A4} on another. No single value has quorum.
vectors.rationale: Split PCR values, no single value backed by quorum. Split-brain attestation → reject. Router MUST NOT choose majority-of-majority; unanimous agreement across present attestors is required per §9.4.

vectors.description: A3 returns all-zero PCRs (misconfigured attestor not actually measuring).
vectors.rationale: All-zero PCRs indicate unreset TPM or bypassed measurement — never a valid measurement for a loaded enclave. §9.4 unanimous-match invariant broken.

vectors.description: PCR0 (boot firmware) differs across two Nitro instances (A1 vs A2) but PCR8 matches. Open spec question: is firmware-level difference tolerated? This suite treats any PCR divergence as reject pending spec clarification.
vectors.rationale: OPEN SPEC QUESTION flagged in notes: §9.4 says 'independently computed matching PCRs' without per-PCR-index tolerance. In real Nitro Enclaves, PCR0 can legitimately differ across hosts. Reference implementation must specify which PCR indices are normative. This suite errs on reject (conservative) until spec clarifies.

vectors.description: Attestor signatures present but transparency_log_proof field is absent.
vectors.rationale: §9.4: 'Transparency-log inclusion proofs' is a mandatory field. Absence defeats the public-auditability property that distinguishes automated attestation from rubber-stamp attestation.

vectors.description: Transparency_log_proof object present but inclusion_proof field itself is null/empty.
vectors.rationale: Empty inclusion_proof is equivalent to missing. Parser must not silently accept nulls.

vectors.description: Inclusion proof present but does not verify against declared log root.
vectors.rationale: Proof fails Merkle verification. Attestation is not actually in the log; public auditability broken. §9.4.

vectors.description: Inclusion proof verifies, but log root is older than policy (root_age_seconds > 86400, i.e., >24h).
vectors.rationale: Stale root enables freeze attacks where adversary isolates Router from fresh log state. Policy default 24h chosen to match CT/Rekor defaults. Spec §9.4 should pin this explicitly.

vectors.description: Proof references log_id 'rogue-log-v1' not in Tariff's trusted_transparency_logs set.
vectors.rationale: Adversary stands up their own log and serves proofs against it. Tariff MUST pin trusted log set. §9.4 + trusted-log enumeration.

vectors.description: Log reports entry submitted but latest signed tree head does not yet cover it.
vectors.rationale: entry_index (98765) > sth_tree_size (50000). Entry exists only in unlogged/promise state; not publicly auditable yet. Mirrors CT SCT-vs-STH distinction.

vectors.description: Attestors unanimously agree on PCR8 value, but that value does not match Tariff's expected_pcrs.PCR8.
vectors.rationale: Attestors agree but agreed-on value is NOT what Tariff declares. Reproducible build verification: the image the attestors built is not the image the Tariff authorizes. §9.3 + §9.4.

vectors.description: Attestors agree on PCR8 but it differs from Tariff's expected_pcrs.PCR8 by a single byte.
vectors.rationale: One-bit flip must reject. Cryptographic hash comparison is not fuzzy. §9.4.

vectors.description: PCR0 (boot firmware) hash not in approved set.
vectors.rationale: Boot-chain divergence → host is untrusted even if application layer matches. Defense against TOCTOU between measurement layers. §9.4.

vectors.description: PCR4 (kernel measurement) deviation.
vectors.rationale: Kernel swap with backdoored variant. Even if application PCR8 matches, untrusted kernel voids guarantees. §9.4.

vectors.description: Attestation matches old Tariff v4's expected_pcrs but a newer Tariff v5 is active with different expected_pcrs.
vectors.rationale: §10.3: Tariff version monotonically increases; latest pinned Tariff is authoritative. Adversary cannot replay an old valid-for-old-Tariff attestation against current Tariff. V3-3 + V3-7 combined defense.

vectors.description: Tariff has classifier_wasm_hash but no expected_pcrs field. Open spec question flagged.
vectors.rationale: OPEN SPEC QUESTION: §2.2 lists `signer_image_pcr_set` as a top-level Tariff field. If absent, the Tariff is malformed and must fail-closed. Router cannot verify arbitrary PCRs against an undefined expectation. Fail closed > fail open.

vectors.description: Bundle bytes do not decode as valid CBOR.
vectors.rationale: Decoder must fail closed on malformed CBOR. Implementers: DO NOT attempt lenient parsing. RFC 8949 §4.2 deterministic-decoding compliance.

vectors.description: Attestation entry missing attestor_id field.
vectors.rationale: Schema requires attestor_id to bind signature to claimed identity. Missing → parse failure. §9.4.

vectors.description: Attestation entry missing iat timestamp field.
vectors.rationale: Without iat, freshness check is impossible. Missing required timestamp → malformed. §9.4.

vectors.description: Bundle contains an unknown field 'attestor_comment' in strict-mode decoding.
vectors.rationale: Strict-mode rejects unknown fields to prevent smuggling channels and ensure forward-compat requires explicit versioning. Matches schema.json additionalProperties:false practice. §9.4.

vectors.description: Bundle reports PCR99 (out of the TPM 2.0 index range [0,23]).
vectors.rationale: TPM 2.0 PCR indices are [0,23]; Nitro exposes [0,15]. PCR99 is structurally impossible and a red flag for custom injection. §9.4.

vectors.description: Attestation iat is 48 hours before current_time; policy default is 24h.
vectors.rationale: Stale attestation could predate a known compromise or revocation. Freshness window analogous to §5.1 mandate exp. Spec should formalize attestation_max_age.

vectors.description: Attestation iat is exactly at the 24h boundary — boundary condition test.
vectors.rationale: 1714525200 - 1714438700 = 86500 > 86400 by 100 seconds. Boundary-sensitive check. Inclusive lower bound is accept; exceeding limit by any amount rejects.

vectors.description: Attestation iat is in the future relative to Router's current_time (clock skew or forgery).
vectors.rationale: iat 24h in future of Router clock. Clock-skew tolerance typically ±60s; 24h is adversarial forgery. Reject to prevent pre-signing attacks.

vectors.description: Attestation iat is BEFORE the Tariff's issued_at; the attestation cannot have attested this Tariff's image.
vectors.rationale: Temporal impossibility: attestation cannot have been made for an image described by a later Tariff. Even if PCRs happen to match, the causality chain is broken.

vectors.description: Router nonce 'r40' was issued and consumed in a prior attestation cycle; attacker replays a bundle referencing it.
vectors.rationale: Replay defense. Nonces are single-use. OPEN SPEC QUESTION: §9.4 does not mandate nonce binding; reference implementation should specify single-use router-supplied nonces.

vectors.description: Adversary replays a valid-looking attestation from 6 months ago when Signer image was different.
vectors.rationale: Classical replay. Both freshness AND nonce-match would reject; expired fires first by convention. V3-3 adversary scenario.

vectors.description: Attestation nonce 'other-nonce' was never issued by this Router.
vectors.rationale: Attestation bound to nonce never issued. Could be a captured attestation for another Router instance or a fabricated one. Nonce-match is mandatory channel binding.

vectors.description: Attestor reports PCRs that match Tariff expected values perfectly, but attestors disagree — one attestor is lying (faking PCRs from a non-TPM source) while others report truthfully.
vectors.rationale: A1 lying (claiming the good PCR) cannot overcome A2+A3's truthful measurement of the actual (backdoored) image. Cross-attestor mismatch catches rehydration. If attackers fake in ALL attestors, only reproducible-build gap remains — but then transparency log + attestor diversity (different infra) raises bar.

vectors.description: Attacker compromises attestor A1's key and signs an attestation for a malicious image. Other attestors are honest and don't sign. Quorum=3 required; only 1 signature present.
vectors.rationale: Stolen single key cannot cross quorum threshold. Attacker also needed to compromise 2 more attestors to succeed — the structural defense of multi-party attestation. V3-3 core.

vectors.description: Adversary serves Router a log root different from what honest clients see. Without witness cosignatures, detection is hard.
vectors.rationale: OPEN PROBLEM: split-view attacks on transparency logs are fundamentally hard without gossip/witness protocols. Spec should reference CT-style witness cosignatures (e.g., RFC 9162 §5.3) for full defense. This vector models a Tariff requiring witnesses; absence of them triggers transparency-invalid. Without witnesses, attack remains feasible for a strong adversary — flagged as residual.

vectors.description: Bundle is zero bytes.
vectors.rationale: Zero bytes is not valid CBOR. Trivially reject; defends against uninitialized-buffer-reads and garbage-in conditions.

vectors.description: Bundle is 10MB of valid CBOR — DoS via decoder resource exhaustion.

input.attestation_bundle_summary: 10MB payload composed of padding within attestations[].padding field

vectors.rationale: Size ceiling (default 1MiB) prevents decoder DoS and parser-state-exhaustion attacks. Enforce BEFORE full CBOR decode. Spec-level default TBD.

vectors.description: Tariff declares quorum=1 and bundle has 1 attestor. Format is valid but this effectively eliminates multi-party attestation.
vectors.rationale: OPEN SPEC QUESTION: §2.2 states 'min 3; quorum 2-of-3' as the normative baseline. A quorum=1 Tariff superficially parses but defeats V3-3 multi-party defense. This suite rejects at the Tariff-validation layer. If spec permits quorum=1 for MV-0/MV-1 deployments, change expected to accept and narrow the reject code.

vectors.description: Same bundle content submitted twice: once with attestations [A1, A2, A3] and once with [A3, A2, A1]. Both must produce identical result. This vector documents the intended invariant (order-independence) via a successful-parse case then checks an alternative ordering expects the same reject reason for an unrelated defect so that order doesn't mask behavior.
vectors.rationale: Same PCR mismatch must be detected regardless of attestation order. Implementations MUST NOT depend on input order for correctness (deterministic set semantics). Contrast with pcrrej-014 which has identical content in canonical order — same reject code.
